reforms.description: The Icelandic government has been negotiating for the construction of a large aluminium smelter in Reydarfjordur and a related power plant in Karahnjukar, which is expected to significantly impact Iceland's economy through increased investment and job creation.
reforms.package_name: Aluminium Smelter and Power Plant Project
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The project is expected to create jobs and attract investment, contributing positively to the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has been negotiating for the construction.
reforms.importance_rationale: The project represents a significant investment and development initiative.
reforms.source_quote: In May 2000, the government signed a declaration with domestic and Norwegian investors regarding the construction of a large aluminium smelter in Reydarfjordur and a related power plant in Karahnjukar.

reforms.description: The Icelandic government granted an exclusive 12-year licence to DeCODE Genetics to create and manage an Icelandic Health Database, which will contain the medical records of most inhabitants in a non-identifiable form, facilitating research in genetics and health.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The database will enhance research capabilities in genetics and health, potentially leading to innovations in medical research.
reforms.status_evidence: The government granted an exclusive licence.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a major step in health research and data management.
reforms.source_quote: After a long public debate, the Icelandic government granted an exclusive 12-year licence to the Icelandic subsidiary of DeCODE Genetics to create and manage an Icelandic Health Database.
